info.title: Operations Center — Equipment Measurements API
info.description:
Submit telematics-style **measurements** (speed, GPS, engine state, fuel, and related fields) for **third-party–managed** equipment created through the Operations Center Equipment POST API. Call at the interval your application chooses; accepted payloads return **204 No Content** and typically appear on the Operations Center map within about **30 seconds**.

**Measurements** here means metadata for equipment that is **not** John Deere–connected terminal telematics—equipment created by end users or partner apps in Operations Center.

tags.name: Equipment measurements
tags.description: Contribute measurement batches for organization equipment.

OAuth2AuthorizationCode.description: Three-legged OAuth 2.0 (authorization code) for John Deere Precision Tech APIs. This endpoint requires **`eq2`**. Request `offline_access` if you need refresh tokens. Discover endpoints from the well-known URL.

scopes.eq2: Equipment Access Level 2 — edit equipment and contribute measurements

MeasurementItem.description: A single measurement. **`name`** identifies the field; **`value`** must use units your client converts correctly before sending. Include **`unit`** where the API defines one.

name.description: Measurement identifier. Typical values: `vehicleSpeed`, `heading`, `fuelLevelPercentage`, `latitude`, `longitude`, `engineState`, `odometer`, `engineHours`, `engineSpeed`, `ptoStatus` (match documented spelling for each field).

value.description: Numeric reading, or strings such as `On`/`Off` for `engineState`, or `On`/`Off`/`Fault`/`Unavailable` for `ptoStatus`.

unit.description: Unit for interpretation. Examples: `kph` (vehicle speed), `degrees` (heading, latitude, longitude), `percent` (fuel), `km` (odometer), `hours` (engine hours), `RPM` or `rpm` (engine speed).

EquipmentMeasurementBatch.description: One timestamped group of measurements. **`timestamp`** orders batches if they arrive out of order.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 time when this set of measurements was recorded.

measurements.description: Measurements for this instant (speed, GPS, engine, etc.).

EquipmentMeasurementsRequestBody.description: One or more timestamped measurement groups. Typically one object per POST.

post.description:
Sends metadata for a **third-party–managed** machine or implement in Operations Center. Use the equipment **`principalId`** from **GET** `/isg/equipment` (or the equipment resource after create). Measurements apply only in the equipment’s **current controlling organization**; the API verifies app and user access to that organization.

**204 No Content** means the batch was accepted for processing.

**OAuth scope:** `eq2`

**Request URL:** `POST https://equipmentapi.deere.com/isg/organizations/{organizationId}/equipment/{principalId}/measurements`

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Operations Center organization id that controls the equipment.

parameters.name: principalId
parameters.description: Equipment **principal id** (master record id) from the equipment record. Required—requests without the correct id fail.

204.description: Success — measurement batch accepted for processing. No response body.

400.description: Invalid payload: malformed JSON, values that cannot be mapped to the expected types, missing or wrong organization/equipment, or equipment that is **John Deere terminal telematics–managed** (measurements cannot be contributed by other means). See response body for details.

403.description: Forbidden — **license**: no API license for this API, or token missing required scopes (ensure **`eq2`** on the bearer token). **Permission**: client or user lacks access to the organization that controls the equipment. Verify: (1) your client has a connection to the organization, (2) the user on the token can access the organization, (3) connection permissions are at least **equipment level 2** in Operations Center.
